title: CitationMode
description: The presentation mode of a citation.
$comment:
This is an enumeration used in Stencila Schema for citation presentation modes.

It exists so citations can preserve the distinction between parenthetical and
narrative forms, including variants that expose only the author or only the
year in surrounding prose. This helps Stencila map citation semantics
consistently across authoring formats, renderers, and citation processors.

See [`Citation.citationMode`](./citation.md#citationmode) for the property
that uses this enumeration.

analogues.name: CSL citation position and narrative conventions
analogues.notes: Close analogue for distinctions such as parenthetical versus narrative citation rendering, though Stencila exposes them as a compact schema enumeration.

type.$comment: This is a special property analogous to JSON-LD's `@type` keyword.

id.$comment: This is a special property analogous to JSON-LD's `@id` keyword.

identifiers.description: Any kind of identifier for any kind of Thing.
identifiers.$comment:
Some identifiers have specific properties e.g the `issn` property for
the `Periodical` type. These should be used in preference to this
property which is intended for identifiers that do not yet have a
specific property. Identifiers can be represented as strings, but
using a `PropertyValue` will usually be better because it allows
for `propertyID` (i.e. the type of identifier).
